citation: LaQue F.L. (1975) Marine Corrosion: Causes and Prevention, Wiley, Tab.6.1 — free-corrosion potential of carbon steel in flowing aerated natural seawater at ambient T.
Stansbury E.E. & Buchanan R.A. (2000) Fundamentals of Electrochemical Corrosion, ASM International, §4.4 + Fig.4-12 — mild steel in aerated fresh water at 25 °C.
NACE SP0169-2013 §6.2 + Romanoff M. (1957) NBS Circular 579 Underground Corrosion — free-corrosion potential of bare carbon steel in moist soil (Cu/CuSO4 reference, CP convention).
Jones D.A. (1996) Principles and Prevention of Corrosion 2nd ed, Prentice Hall, §3.4 Ex.3.4 — Fe in 1N H2SO4 aerated: i_corr ~120 µA/cm² → 1.4 mm/yr; Tafel slopes from Fig.3-13.
Stansbury & Buchanan (2000) §5.6 Fig.5-12 — mild steel in 1N HCl at 25 °C, mixed-potential analysis.
Sedriks A.J. (1996) Corrosion of Stainless Steels 2nd ed, Wiley/ECS, Tab.4-5 + Fig.4-11 — 304 SS in aerated seawater 25 °C: passive current 0.1 µA/cm², E_pit ~0.3 V Ag/AgCl_sat.
Sedriks (1996) Fig.5-7 — anodic polarisation of 304 SS in 1N H2SO4 at 30 °C: E_corr ~-0.25 V SCE, passive plateau ~2.5 µA/cm².
Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 + Fig.4-11 — 316L SS in aerated seawater 25 °C: passive plateau 0.05 µA/cm², E_pit ~0.45 V Ag/AgCl_sat.
Sedriks (1996) Fig.5-9 — 316L in 1N H2SO4 at 30 °C, passive plateau ~1.5 µA/cm² (Mo lowers it vs 304).
Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 — 2205 duplex SS (S32205) in aerated seawater 25 °C: passive plateau 0.02 µA/cm², E_pit ~0.9 V Ag/AgCl_sat (PREN_N30 35).
Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 + Nilsson J.-O. (1992) Mater. Sci. Tech. 8, 685 — 2507 super-duplex (S32750, PREN_N30 42) in aerated seawater 25 °C.
Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 — 254SMO / S31254 6-Mo super-austenitic (PREN_N30 43) in aerated seawater.
Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 — 904L super-austenitic (N08904, PREN_N30 35) in aerated seawater 25 °C.

ASTM G5-14 Annex A1 — Standard Reference Test Method for Making Potentiodynamic Anodic Polarization Measurements: 430 SS in 1N H2SO4 at 30 °C round-robin: E_corr -0.522 ± 0.027 V SCE; i_pass 1.8 ± 0.9 µA/cm².
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Inconel 625 chapter + Sedriks (1996) Tab.4-5 — Alloy 625 in aerated seawater 25 °C: PREN_N30 ~52, virtually immune to pitting / crevice up to ~80 °C.
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Hastelloy C-276 chapter — Ni-Cr-Mo (PREN ~70) in flowing aerated seawater: passive currents <0.01 µA/cm², no pitting observed in 5-year LaQue Beach exposure.
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Incoloy 825 chapter — Ni-Fe-Cr-Mo (PREN_N30 ~32) in aerated seawater 25 °C: passive, E_pit ~0.45 V (susceptible to crevice in stagnant).

Schutz R.W. & Thomas D.E. (1987) ASM Handbook v.13 Corrosion 1st ed — Ti & Ti alloys; LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — CP Ti Gr 2 in seawater: stable passive, no pitting below ~80 °C, E_pit > 8 V (transpassive).
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — Cu in flowing aerated seawater: E_corr -0.30 V Ag/AgCl, low CR (~0.05 mm/yr); Stansbury (2000) §4.5 Fig.4-22 mixed-potential.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — C70600 (90-10 Cu-Ni) in flowing seawater 25 °C: E_corr -0.28 V Ag/AgCl, condenser-tube workhorse. Schussler M. (1985) Copper Development Association TN-31.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — C71500 (70-30 Cu-Ni) in flowing aerated seawater 25 °C.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — C95800 Nickel-Al-bronze (NAB) in flowing aerated seawater 25 °C; common ship-propeller alloy.

source: LaQue 1975 Tab.6.1 + Davis 1999 ASM Al Alloy Corrosion
citation: LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1; Davis J.R. (1999) Corrosion of Aluminum and Aluminum Alloys, ASM International — 6061-T6 in flowing aerated seawater: E_corr -0.85 V Ag/AgCl, pits in Cl-rich solutions above E_pit ~-0.75 V.
Davis (1999) Corrosion of Al ASM — 5083-H116 marine plate (Mg ~4.5%) in flowing aerated seawater: marine workhorse; ABS-approved hull plate.

source: Davis 1999 ASM Al Alloy Corrosion Tab.21
citation: Davis (1999) Corrosion of Al ASM Tab.21 — 2024-T3 (4.4Cu, aerospace) in seawater: Cu pulls E_corr more noble vs 5xxx; intergranular SCC susceptible.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — Zn in flowing seawater 25 °C: E_corr -1.03 V Ag/AgCl, ~12 kg/A·yr consumption (DNV-RP-B401 Tab.7-1 ε_th 780 A·h/kg).
DNV-RP-B401:2017 Tab.7-1 — Al-Zn-In sacrificial anode (5Zn + ~0.02In): E°_anode -1.05 V Ag/AgCl seawater, ε_th 2700 A·h/kg (= 3.25 kg/A·yr at 80% utilisation).
Pourbaix M. (1974) Atlas of Electrochemical Equilibria in Aqueous Solutions, NACE, §15 Mg — E_Mg/Mg2+ -2.36 V SHE; NACE SP0572-2007 ε_th 1230 A·h/kg for AZ-63 anode (H-1 grade per ASTM B843).
Romanoff M. (1957) Underground Corrosion, NBS Circular 579 — gray cast iron in soil, 10-15 yr in-service E_corr typical band.
NACE SP0169-2013 §6.2 + Beavers J.A. (2014) Mater. Perf. — bare X65 pipeline steel in moist neutral soil: -0.55 V CSE consistent CP criterion baseline (free-corrosion before CP).
Porter F.C. (1991) Corrosion Resistance of Zinc and Zinc Alloys, CRC — hot-dip-galvanised steel in soft fresh water 25 °C; Zn layer governs E_corr until consumed.
Stansbury & Buchanan (2000) §4.4 Fig.4-12 — mild steel in 3.5% NaCl aerated 25 °C, ~0.21 mm/yr.
Sedriks (1996) Fig.4-15 — 316L in 3.5% NaCl deaerated 25 °C: passive, E_pit ~0.4 V SCE.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1; ASM v.13B (2005) Monel chapter — Monel 400 (Ni-Cu) in flowing seawater 25 °C: passive but crevice-susceptible.
Uhlig H.H. & Revie R.W. (1985) Corrosion and Corrosion Control 3rd ed, Wiley, Tab.8.1 — Pb in flowing aerated seawater 25 °C (PbSO4 film).
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Wrought stainless steels — N08904 chapter; Sedriks (1996) Fig.5-11 — 904L iso-corrosion 0.1 mm/yr line for H2SO4 covers 50 °C / 1N.
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Inconel 625 chapter Tab.3 — N06625 in 1N HCl 90 °C: passive with i_pass <0.5 µA/cm² (Mo-stabilised passive film).
Haynes International (2017) Hastelloy C-276 Brochure H-2002F Tab.4 — N10276 iso-corrosion 0.1 mm/yr boundary: 1N HCl ~115 °C; ASM v.13B C-276 chapter.
Haynes International (2017) Hastelloy C-276 Brochure H-2002F Tab.4 — N10276 iso-corrosion in H2SO4: 70 wt% boundary 60 °C; well within passive at 1N (~10 wt%) 90 °C.
Haynes International Hastelloy C-22 Brochure H-2019 — N06022 (Cr-Mo-W) ranks above C-276 in reducing-acid chloride: 1N HCl 90 °C passive at <0.1 µA/cm².
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Hastelloy C-22 chapter — N06022 in flowing aerated seawater 25 °C: PREN ~67, virtually immune to pitting/crevice.

API RP 945 3rd ed (2003) §5.3 — carbon steel in lean MDEA 50 °C; Bagdasarian A. et al. (1991) Mater. Perf. 30(5), 63 — general corrosion ~0.025-0.075 mm/yr; alkaline → low CR but ANCC SCC concern above 60 °C.
API RP 945 (2003) §6 + Sedriks (1996) Fig.5-15 — 316L in lean MDEA / DEA 40-80 °C: passive; heat-stable salts and HSAS push E_pit down. Interpolated within Sedriks cited band.

NACE TM0177-2016 §A NACE Solution A (5 wt% NaCl + 0.5 wt% acetic acid + H2S sat) — laboratory standard; Bockris J.O'M. et al. (1961) Electrochim. Acta 4, 325 — Fe in H2S solution: cathodic depolarisation by H2S, i_corr 50-100 µA/cm².
ISO 15156-3:2020 Tab.A.6 (13Cr S41426/S41427) + Marchebois H. et al. (2003) Corrosion 59, 1063 — 13Cr in sour CO2/H2S brine 90 °C: passive but E_pit only 0.15 V (vs >0.4 V sweet).
ISO 15156-3:2020 Tab.A.27 (22Cr duplex) + Linne C.P. et al. (2000) NACE Corrosion 2000 Paper 00136 — 2205 in sour brine 60 °C: max 1 bar H2S at pH ≥3.5, hardness limit 28 HRC.
ISO 15156-3:2020 Tab.A.36 (N08825) + Rhodes P.R. (2007) Corrosion 63, 63 — Alloy 825 in sour HPHT brine ≤180 °C, ≤200 kPa H2S, ≤17% Cl.
ISO 15156-3:2020 Tab.A.39 (N06625) + Smith L.M. et al. (1994) Corrosion 50, 156 — Inconel 625 cold-worked in sour HPHT to 232 °C, partial H2S limits.
de Waard C. & Milliams D.E. (1975) Corrosion 31, 177 — Fe in CO2-saturated brine 60 °C / 1 bar pCO2: CR ~1.2 mm/yr (100 µA/cm²); NORSOK M-506:2017 reproduces.
Ueda M. & Takabe H. (1999) NACE Corrosion 99 Paper 99587 — 13Cr (S41425/L80) in sweet CO2 brine 90-150 °C: passive at <2 µA/cm² up to 200 °C in absence of H2S.
Dugstad A. (2003) NACE Corrosion 2003 Paper 03319; Halvorsen A.M. et al. (2000) IFE OG36 — CS in lean MEG 25 °C (gas-export glycol injection): CR ~0.02-0.05 mm/yr at pH 7.5; sharply rises if pH <6.
Halvorsen A.M. et al. (2007) NACE Corrosion 2007 Paper 07484 — 316L in lean MEG / 500 ppm Cl 25 °C: passive, no pitting in 6-mo loop test.
NACE SP0403-2015 Caustic Service (avoiding caustic SCC) + Pourbaix (1974) Atlas §10 Fe — CS in 50 wt% NaOH 60 °C: low general CR but caustic-SCC risk above 50 °C / NACE Tab.1.
Sedriks (1996) §5.7 + Crum J.R. (1981) Corrosion 37, 633 — 316L in 50% NaOH 80 °C: passive with low rate; caustic-SCC above 110 °C in chloride-free; Cl shifts boundary down.
Crum J.R. (1981) Corrosion 37, 633 — Ni-Cr-Mo alloys in 50% NaOH 100 °C: Alloy 625 immune to caustic SCC up to 150 °C in absence of chlorine compounds.
ISO 9223:2012 Tab.3 (corrosivity category C5 marine industrial) + ASTM G50-10 — bare CS in coastal marine atmosphere: first-year CR ~80-200 µm (8 µA/cm² equiv); declines with rust layer.
ISO 9223:2012 Tab.3 (corrosivity category C4 high — urban / industrial with SO2): CS first-year CR 50-80 µm.
ISO 9223:2012 Tab.3 (corrosivity category C2 low — rural with brief wetting): CS first-year CR 1.3-25 µm.

source: Cardarelli 2008 Mater Handbook §11 + Cabot 2001 Ta brochure
citation: Cardarelli F. (2008) Materials Handbook §11 (refractory metals) + Cabot Specialty Metals (2001) Tantalum brochure — Ta in boiling 37% HCl: immune; passive film Ta2O5 stable to >200 °C; widely used as bursting-disc material in HCl scrubbers.
Cardarelli (2008) Materials Handbook §11 — Ta in boiling 98% H2SO4: immune up to 175 °C; reacts above 200 °C; widely used in pickling-line linings.
Cardarelli (2008) §11 + Kapur D.M.M. (2000) Mater. Sci. Eng. A 282, 178 — Nb in 20% HCl 60 °C: passive at <0.05 mm/yr; cheaper Ta-substitute for moderate HCl service.
Schutz R.W. & Thomas D.E. (1987) ASM Handbook v.13 Corrosion 1st ed — CP Ti in 1N H2SO4 25 °C: active dissolution (no passive film stable in reducing acid without oxidiser); CR ~0.9 mm/yr.
Schutz & Thomas (1987) ASM v.13 Ti — Ti in 50% HNO3 60 °C: passive film TiO2 stabilised by nitrate oxidiser, CR <0.025 mm/yr; immune to red-fuming HNO3 (RFNA) below 1.34 SG.
TIMET (2000) TICORR data sheet — Ti Gr 7 (Ti-0.15Pd) in 10% H2SO4 80 °C: Pd cathodic-modifier shifts Ti from active to passive branch (E_corr +350 mV vs Gr 2); CR <0.025 mm/yr.
Schussler M. (1985) Copper Development Association TN-31 + AWWA C800 — Cu in potable / boiler-feed water 25 °C: ~0.005-0.025 mm/yr in well-buffered FW pH 7-8.5.
Stansbury & Buchanan (2000) §4.5 Fig.4-19 — Cu in deaerated 1N HCl 25 °C: ~0.5 mm/yr (CuCl complex).
Copper Development Association Pub. AB-100 — NAB C95800 in fresh water 25 °C: similar low CR to pure Cu, used as marine-pump impeller.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — Naval brass C46400 (60Cu-39Zn-1Sn) in flowing seawater 25 °C: Sn inhibits dezincification; CR ~0.05 mm/yr.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — Yellow brass C26800 (65Cu-35Zn) in seawater: dezincification problem; not recommended for marine without additives (use admiralty / aluminium brass).
Schussler (1985) Copper Development Association TN-31 — Aluminium brass C68700 (76Cu-22Zn-2Al) condenser tube in seawater: Al-enriched film resists impingement; CR ~0.02 mm/yr at 1-2 m/s.
Brush Wellman (now Materion) (1998) Tech Bull. AT-0029 — C17200 BeCu in seawater 25 °C: comparable to other bronzes; downhole spring service.
Davis (1999) Corrosion of Al & Al Alloys ASM, Tab.18 — Al 1100 (commercially pure) in flowing aerated seawater 25 °C: pits in chloride but no SCC; CR ~0.025 mm/yr.
Davis (1999) Tab.18 — Al 3003 (Mn-bearing) in seawater: heat-exchanger tube alloy; same E_corr as 1100.
Davis (1999) Corrosion of Al ASM + Aluminum Association (2009) Aluminium Design Manual — 5052 (Mg ~2.5%) marine sheet: balance of strength + Cl resistance below 5083.
Davis (1999) Tab.18 — Al 7075-T6 (Zn-Mg-Cu aerospace) in seawater: SCC-susceptible (Stress-Corrosion in 7xxx), exfoliation per ASTM G34.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — Gray cast iron in seawater: graphitic corrosion (Fe selectively dissolves leaving graphite skeleton); CR ~0.15 mm/yr.
NACE SP0169-2013 §6.2 — Class 1 / 2 pressure-vessel plate in moist soil; same baseline as generic CS.
Sedriks (1996) Fig.4-13 — 13Cr martensitic SS (S41000) in aerated seawater 25 °C: only marginally passive, E_pit near E_corr → pits readily in stagnant SW.
ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) Precipitation-Hardening SS chapter — 17-4PH (S17400) H1025 in seawater: slightly better than 13Cr (Nb-Cu mod), still susceptible to SCC in stagnant chloride.
Sedriks (1996) Fig.4-15 — 904L in 3.5% NaCl 25 °C: superior to 316L by 250-300 mV in E_pit, common HCl/sulfuric service alternative.
Outokumpu Stainless (2017) Data Sheet 254SMO — S31254 in 3.5% NaCl 50 °C: E_pit ~0.9 V SCE; CCT >80 °C in 6% FeCl3 (ASTM G48 Method A).

IAPWS TGD2-09 (2015) Technical Guidance Doc. on Power-Cycle Chemistry + ASME PTC 31.1 — CS economiser tube in AVT-controlled boiler feedwater 200 °C / pH 9.5 (NH3): magnetite forms protective Fe3O4 film; CR <0.01 mm/yr.
Sim S. et al. (2014) Corrosion 70, 185; Choi Y.-S. et al. (2010) Corrosion 66, 015002 — CS in supercritical CO2 + water-phase 100 °C / 100 bar: CR ~0.4 mm/yr; CCS pipeline material concern.

Wei L. et al. (2015) Int. J. Greenhouse Gas Control 35, 145; Hua Y. et al. (2017) Mater. Corros. 68, 56 — 316L in scCO2 100 °C / 100 bar: CR <0.001 mm/yr in water-free, low passive current with O2 < 100 ppm.
API RP 751 4th ed (2013) §A; NACE 5A192-2009 — CS in anhydrous HF alkylation 40 °C: protective FeF2 scale; CR <0.025 mm/yr in clean dry HF; HF-SCC risk with PWHT > 80 HRB hardness.
NACE 5A192 + Special Metals (2005) Monel 400 — N04400 in 48% HF 80 °C: CR <0.02 mm/yr; Ni-Cu alloy preferred over CS for HF alkylation reactor internals where FeF2 scale unreliable.
Sedriks (1996) §5.3 — CS in dilute HNO3 25 °C: extreme attack (~60 mm/yr in 30 wt% HNO3); never used; CS becomes passive only in fuming HNO3 (>90 wt%) due to thin nitrate film.
Sedriks (1996) §5.3 — 304 in 65% HNO3 50 °C: passive at 0.5 µA/cm² (Huey test ASTM A262 Practice C); choice alloy for nitric service unless sensitised.
ASTM B473 + ASM Handbook v.13B (2005) — Alloy 20 (CN-7M, 20Cr-29Ni-Cu-Mo) in 60% H2SO4 80 °C: workhorse acid alloy, CR <0.05 mm/yr.
Stansbury & Buchanan (2000) Fig.4-12 — CS in fresh water at 80 °C (hot-water service): CR ~0.25 mm/yr; ASHRAE Handbook (2015) HVAC Systems Ch.49 baseline.
Sedriks (1996) §4.3 + ASTM A380 — 316L in potable / boiler-feed FW 25 °C: passive; deionised water below 0.05 µS/cm can cause crevice in dead-leg per ASME BPE 2019.
Porter F.C. (1991) Corrosion of Zinc CRC — Zn in soft FW 25 °C: ~0.025 mm/yr; rises sharply in pH<6 or pH>12 (amphoteric).
Song G. & Atrens A. (2007) Adv. Eng. Mater. 9, 177 — Mg in 3.5% NaCl 25 °C: ~6 mm/yr; not used as structural in marine but high-current anode (~1230 A·h/kg via NACE SP0572).
MIL-DTL-18001K (2014) Anodes, Corrosion Preventive, Zinc; DNV-RP-B401:2017 Tab.7-1 — Zn anode: ε_th 780 A·h/kg, working potential -1.03 V Ag/AgCl_SW, util 0.90.
Haynes International Hastelloy G-30 Brochure H-2024 — N06030 (Cr-Mo-W-Fe with N) in 50% H2SO4 90 °C: passive plateau <0.5 µA/cm²; phosphoric-acid plant alloy.
Sedriks (1996) §5.6 Fig.5-22 — 316L in 75% H3PO4 80 °C: passive at <5 µA/cm² in pure phosphoric; sharp rise above ~85% conc or with Cl/F contamination (fertilizer-grade WPA breakdown).
ASTM B463 + Sedriks (1996) — Alloy 20 in WPA (wet-process phosphoric acid, contains ~1 g/L Cl + 2 g/L F) 100 °C: workhorse alloy, CR 0.05-0.15 mm/yr.

IAPWS TGD2-09 (2015); EPRI 1014099 (2006) Boiler & HRSG Tube Failures — CS economiser at 300 °C: passive magnetite film, FAC risk at locations with low oxidising potential (single-phase 90-230 °C critical band).
EPRI 3002013108 (2018) PWSCC Database; Scott P.M. (2000) Corrosion 56, 771 — Alloy 600 in PWR primary water 290 °C: low general CR but PWSCC of mill-annealed material (Cr-depleted grain boundaries).

EPRI 3002013108 (2018) — Alloy 690 (30 Cr, replacement for 600 in steam-generator tubes) in PWR primary water 320 °C: factor >30 improvement in PWSCC initiation time vs 600 (Norring 2008 Mater Sci Tech).

source: Cooling Tower Institute CTI ESG-200 + ASTM D1384
citation: Cooling Technology Institute CTI ESG-200 (2018) — CS in inhibitor-treated open cooling water 35 °C: CR target <0.075 mm/yr with phosphonate + Zn + tolyl-triazole; ASTM D1384 simulant.
ASM v.13B + Crum J.R. (1989) Mater. Perf. 28(2), 53 — C-276 in wet limestone FGD scrubber sump 60 °C / 5% Cl / pH 2.5: passive; chosen over 317L when Cl >20000 ppm.
Smith L.M. (1990) NACE Corrosion 90 Paper 90139 — Alloy 625 weld overlay on CS in FGD absorber 60 °C / pH 4 / 3% Cl: common construction; CR <0.01 mm/yr.
MIL-STD-1500 + AMS-QQ-P-416 (2011) Cd plating — Cd on steel in marine atmosphere: sacrificial to steel but more noble than Zn; aerospace fastener use restricted by ECHA REACH.
LaQue (1975) Tab.6.1 — C51000 phosphor bronze (Cu-5Sn-0.2P) in seawater: spring / bushing alloy; Sn-stabilised film.
Copper Development Association Pub. AB-100 — C86500 Mn bronze (high-strength brass, 58Cu-Mn-Al-Sn-Zn) in seawater: ship propeller alloy until displaced by NAB.

Bertolini L. et al. (2013) Corrosion of Steel in Concrete 2nd ed, Wiley-VCH + ACI 222R-19 — black-bar rebar in chloride-free concrete pore solution (Ca(OH)2 sat, pH 12.6): passive; pitting risk at Cl/OH >0.6 (Hausmann 1967).
Bertolini et al. (2013) Tab.6.2 — rebar in carbonated concrete (pH 9, atmospheric CO2 ingress): passive film unstable; CR ~0.03 mm/yr in atmospheric exposure, scales with RH.
